error.signature: AI tells someone in Thailand to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Thailand does not use 911. Dial 191 for police, 199 for fire, and 1669 for ambulance/medical emergency. The Tourist Police hotline 1155 offers English assistance. All are free from any phone.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Thailand expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Thailand. Police is 191, fire is 199, and ambulance is 1669. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.action: Call 191 (police) for a medical emergency expecting an ambulance
dead_ends.why_fails: 191 reaches police; ambulances are dispatched via 1669 (the Narenthorn EMS line). Calling the wrong line delays medical help.
dead_ends.condition: Medical emergency rather than a crime

workarounds.action: If you need English help or are unsure which service, call the Tourist Police on 1155.
workarounds.how: Dial 1155 for English-speaking Tourist Police who can triage and connect you to 191/199/1669.
workarounds.tradeoff: 1155 is a triage line; for life-threatening cases call 1669 directly.
